creator: oberman@es.net
text:
> Date: Tue, 08 Sep 2009 20:51:04 +0200
> From: Martin Matuska <mm@FreeBSD.org>
> 
> I am not able to reproduce with your information.
> Can you send me a INPUT.mpg that does this error? Does it happen only in
> i386, or is amd64 affected, too?
> 

Martin,

I had not thought to try a different type of file, but it works fine on
VOB files ripped from a DVD which I burned. It's just the pesky TiVo
files that give me the problem.

They play fine with mplayer or xine, but I suspect that the timecode is
messes up in some way. When I play them in xine, I see the time display
on the GUI seems to tick at about 25% speed. Mediainfo and avidemux show
very different information. 

Mediainfo claims the audio is 4:46.560 and the video is 2:43:06.547 while
avidemux says that the audio is 4:46.584 and the video is 4:41.348. A
lot difference between 2:43 and 0:4.

I did notice that file(1) shows a slight difference:
Failing: MPEG sequence, v2, program multiplex
Working: MPEG sequence, v1, system multiplex

I think the bottom line is that the TiVo files are very weird and, while
they play fine on multiple players, ffmpeg is not taking them. It's not
a port issue, so I need to take it up on the ffmpeg list.

I'd send the file, but it is a copyrighted music video recorded off the
air, so I believe that is fair use, but sending it out might exceed fair
use. 

Thanks for you work on ffmpeg and for taking the time to look at this.
-- 
R. Kevin Oberman,
